## 3.14 Database Maintenance

```sql
-- Automatic refresh of materialized views
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION refresh_canvas_views()
RETURNS void AS $$
BEGIN
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Schedule refresh every 15 minutes (use pg_cron or external scheduler)
-- SELECT cron.schedule('*/15 * * * *', 'SELECT refresh_canvas_views()');

-- Cleanup expired scenarios
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
DECLARE
    deleted_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    UPDATE scenarios 
    SET status = 'EXPIRED'
    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' 
      AND expires_at < NOW();
    
    GET DIAGNOSTICS deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RETURN deleted_count;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Release expired soft reservations
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
DECLARE
    released_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    -- Update inventory positions
    UPDATE inventory_positions ip
    SET quantity_reserved = quantity_reserved - expired.total_qty
    FROM (
        SELECT inventory_position_id, SUM(quantity_reserved) as total_qty
        FROM material_reservations
        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
          AND expires_at < NOW()
          AND fulfilled_at IS NULL
        GROUP BY inventory_position_id
    ) expired
    WHERE ip.id = expired.inventory_position_id;
    
    -- Delete expired reservations
    DELETE FROM material_reservations
    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
      AND expires_at < NOW()
      AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
    
    GET DIAGNOSTICS released_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RETURN released_count;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Update train target mileage for fleet balancing
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets()
RETURNS void AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE trains t
    SET target_mileage_km = fleet_avg.avg_mileage
    FROM (
        SELECT fleet_id, AVG(current_mileage_km)::INTEGER as avg_mileage
        FROM trains
        WHERE active = TRUE
        GROUP BY fleet_id
    ) fleet_avg
    WHERE t.fleet_id = fleet_avg.fleet_id
      AND t.active = TRUE;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
```

/**
 * MMS Database Enums
 *
 * PostgreSQL enum types for the Metro Fleet Mileage Management System.
 * These enums ensure data integrity and provide type safety across the application.
 *
 * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.2
 */

import { pgEnum } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';

/**
 * Train fitness status - determines operational capability
 * - FIT: Train is fully operational
 * - RESTRICTED: Train has limited capability (e.g., reduced speed)
 * - GROUNDED: Train cannot operate and is awaiting maintenance
 */
export const fitnessStatusEnum = pgEnum('fitness_status', ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED']);

/**
 * PM schedule status - tracks maintenance task lifecycle
 * - SCHEDULED: PM is planned for a future date
 * - IN_PROGRESS: PM is currently being executed
 * - COMPLETED: PM has been finished successfully
 * - CANCELLED: PM was cancelled (with reason)
 * - DEFERRED: PM was postponed to a later date
 */
export const pmStatusEnum = pgEnum('pm_status', [
  'SCHEDULED',
  'IN_PROGRESS',
  'COMPLETED',
  'CANCELLED',
  'DEFERRED',
]);

/**
 * Track type - categorizes maintenance bay capability
 * - LIGHT: Light maintenance (C1)
 * - HEAVY: Heavy maintenance (C2, C3)
 * - OVERHAUL: Major overhaul (C4, OH)
 * - STABLING: Storage only, no maintenance capability
 */
export const trackTypeEnum = pgEnum('track_type', ['LIGHT', 'HEAVY', 'OVERHAUL', 'STABLING']);

/**
 * Work shift type - for man-hour planning
 */
export const shiftTypeEnum = pgEnum('shift_type', ['DAY', 'NIGHT', 'WEEKEND', 'HOLIDAY']);

/**
 * Man-hour adjustment status - workflow states
 */
export const adjustmentStatusEnum = pgEnum('adjustment_status', [
  'PROPOSED',
  'APPROVED',
  'REJECTED',
  'APPLIED',
]);

/**
 * Plan baseline status - snapshot lifecycle
 * - DRAFT: Being prepared, not yet official
 * - PUBLISHED: Active official baseline
 * - SUPERSEDED: Replaced by newer baseline
 * - ARCHIVED: Kept for historical reference
 */
export const baselineStatusEnum = pgEnum('baseline_status', [
  'DRAFT',
  'PUBLISHED',
  'SUPERSEDED',
  'ARCHIVED',
]);

/**
 * Scenario status - what-if analysis states
 * - ACTIVE: Being worked on
 * - PROMOTED: Accepted and converted to baseline
 * - DISCARDED: Rejected
 * - EXPIRED: Auto-expired after 30 days of inactivity
 */
export const scenarioStatusEnum = pgEnum('scenario_status', [
  'ACTIVE',
  'PROMOTED',
  'DISCARDED',
  'EXPIRED',
]);

/**
 * Material criticality - determines scheduling constraints
 * - BLOCKING: PM cannot start without this part
 * - DEFERRABLE: PM can start but needs part within X days
 * - CONSUMABLE: Nice-to-have, can substitute
 */
export const materialCriticalityEnum = pgEnum('material_criticality', [
  'BLOCKING',
  'DEFERRABLE',
  'CONSUMABLE',
]);

/**
 * Reservation type - material allocation commitment level
 * - SOFT: Tentative, can be released if PM moves
 * - HARD: Committed, parts allocated
 */
export const reservationTypeEnum = pgEnum('reservation_type', ['SOFT', 'HARD']);

/**
 * Day type - for service demand and utilization profiles (v2.1)
 */
export const dayTypeEnum = pgEnum('day_type', ['WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY']);

/**
 * Unavailability type - reason for train being out of service (v2.1)
 * - CORRECTIVE: Unplanned maintenance (from Maximo)
 * - INCIDENT: Safety incident requiring inspection
 * - COLLISION: Collision damage
 * - VANDALISM: Intentional damage
 * - EXTERNAL: External factors (weather, infrastructure)
 * - PLANNED_OUTAGE: Scheduled downtime
 */
export const unavailabilityTypeEnum = pgEnum('unavailability_type', [
  'CORRECTIVE',
  'INCIDENT',
  'COLLISION',
  'VANDALISM',
  'EXTERNAL',
  'PLANNED_OUTAGE',
]);

/**
 * Unavailability status (v2.1)
 */
export const unavailabilityStatusEnum = pgEnum('unavailability_status', ['ACTIVE', 'RESOLVED']);

/**
 * Track capability - specific equipment/features (v2.1)
 * Used for constraint-based PM scheduling
 */
export const trackCapabilityEnum = pgEnum('track_capability', [
  'PIT',
  'CRANE',
  'LIFTING_JACKS',
  'WHEEL_LATHE',
  'CATENARY_ISOLATION',
  'ROOF_ACCESS',
  'WASH',
  'DROP_TABLE',
]);

/**
 * Constraint type - technical compatibility constraints (v2.1)
 */
export const constraintTypeEnum = pgEnum('constraint_type', [
  'SIGNALING',
  'GAUGE',
  'POWER_SUPPLY',
  'PLATFORM_LENGTH',
  'MAX_SPEED',
  'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE',
]);

/**
 * Assumption type - for cold start data migration (v2.1)
 * Tracks how PM history was inferred when actual records are missing
 */
export const assumptionTypeEnum = pgEnum('assumption_type', [
  'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING',
  'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM',
  'MANUAL_OVERRIDE',
  'IMPORTED',
  'NO_HISTORY',
]);

/**
 * Confidence level - data quality indicator (v2.1)
 */
export const confidenceLevelEnum = pgEnum('confidence_level', [
  'HIGH',
  'MEDIUM',
  'LOW',
  'ESTIMATED',
]);

/**
 * Validation status - for mileage readings
 */
export const validationStatusEnum = pgEnum('validation_status', [
  'PENDING',
  'VALID',
  'ANOMALY',
  'CORRECTED',
]);

/**
 * Anomaly type - for mileage reading anomalies
 */
export const anomalyTypeEnum = pgEnum('anomaly_type', ['NEGATIVE', 'SPIKE', 'MISSING', 'DUPLICATE']);

/**
 * Meter event type - for odometer events
 */
export const meterEventTypeEnum = pgEnum('meter_event_type', [
  'REPLACEMENT',
  'RESET',
  'CALIBRATION',
  'SWAP',
]);

/**
 * Component status - train component lifecycle
 */
export const componentStatusEnum = pgEnum('component_status', [
  'IN_SERVICE',
  'SPARE',
  'IN_OVERHAUL',
  'CONDEMNED',
]);

/**
 * Removal reason - why a component was removed
 */
export const removalReasonEnum = pgEnum('removal_reason', [
  'SCHEDULED_OVERHAUL',
  'FAILURE',
  'SWAP',
  'UPGRADE',
]);

/**
 * Audit action - for audit log entries
 */
export const auditActionEnum = pgEnum('audit_action', [
  'CREATE',
  'UPDATE',
  'DELETE',
  'APPROVE',
  'REJECT',
  'PUBLISH',
]);

/**
 * Change source - who/what initiated a change
 */
export const changeSourceEnum = pgEnum('change_source', ['USER', 'OPTIMIZER', 'SYSTEM', 'IMPORT']);

/**
 * PM change type - specific PM schedule changes
 */
export const pmChangeTypeEnum = pgEnum('pm_change_type', [
  'CREATED',
  'DATE_CHANGED',
  'STATUS_CHANGED',
  'TRACK_ASSIGNED',
  'LOCKED',
  'UNLOCKED',
  'COMPLETED',
  'CANCELLED',
  'DEFERRED',
]);

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Core Fleet Domain Tables
     3→ *
     4→ * Asset master data: depots, fleets, trains, routes, and tracks.
     5→ * These form the foundation of the fleet management system.
     6→ *
     7→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.2
     8→ */
     9→
    10→import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    11→import {
    12→  boolean,
    13→  date,
    14→  decimal,
    15→  index,
    16→  integer,
    17→  pgTable,
    18→  text,
    19→  timestamp,
    20→  uniqueIndex,
    21→  uuid,
    22→  varchar,
    23→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    24→import { fitnessStatusEnum, trackTypeEnum } from './enums';
    25→
    26→/**
    27→ * Depots - Maintenance facilities
    28→ *
    29→ * Physical locations where trains are maintained and stored.
    30→ * Each depot has a timezone for correct scheduling calculations.
    31→ */
    32→export const depots = pgTable(
    33→  'depots',
    34→  {
    35→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    36→    code: varchar('code', { length: 10 }).notNull().unique(),
    37→    name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    38→    location: varchar('location', { length: 200 }),
    39→    timezone: varchar('timezone', { length: 50 }).default('Asia/Singapore'),
    40→    active: boolean('active').default(true),
    41→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    42→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    43→  },
    44→  (table) => [uniqueIndex('idx_depots_code').on(table.code)]
    45→);
    46→
    47→/**
    48→ * Fleets - Train types/manufacturers
    49→ *
    50→ * A fleet represents a group of similar trains from the same manufacturer.
    51→ * PM types are defined per fleet as maintenance requirements vary by train model.
    52→ */
    53→export const fleets = pgTable(
    54→  'fleets',
    55→  {
    56→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    57→    code: varchar('code', { length: 10 }).notNull().unique(),
    58→    name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    59→    manufacturer: varchar('manufacturer', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    60→    model: varchar('model', { length: 100 }),
    61→    baseDepotId: uuid('base_depot_id').references(() => depots.id),
    62→    carsPerTrain: integer('cars_per_train').default(6),
    63→    active: boolean('active').default(true),
    64→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    65→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    66→  },
    67→  (table) => [uniqueIndex('idx_fleets_code').on(table.code)]
    68→);
    69→
    70→/**
    71→ * Trains - Individual train assets
    72→ *
    73→ * Core asset in the system. Each train belongs to a fleet and accumulates mileage.
    74→ * The fitness_status determines if the train can be scheduled for operations or maintenance.
    75→ *
    76→ * Key fields:
    77→ * - current_mileage_km: Cumulative km reading
    78→ * - target_mileage_km: Calculated target for fleet mileage balancing
    79→ * - mileage_variance_km: Deviation from target (positive = above target)
    80→ */
    81→export const trains = pgTable(
    82→  'trains',
    83→  {
    84→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    85→    fleetId: uuid('fleet_id')
    86→      .notNull()
    87→      .references(() => fleets.id),
    88→    trainNumber: varchar('train_number', { length: 20 }).notNull().unique(),
    89→    commissioningDate: date('commissioning_date'),
    90→    currentMileageKm: integer('current_mileage_km').notNull().default(0),
    91→    fitnessStatus: fitnessStatusEnum('fitness_status').default('FIT'),
    92→    fitnessNotes: text('fitness_notes'),
    93→    currentDepotId: uuid('current_depot_id').references(() => depots.id),
    94→    homeDepotId: uuid('home_depot_id').references(() => depots.id),
    95→    active: boolean('active').default(true),
    96→
    97→    // Mileage balancing - target is calculated from fleet average
    98→    targetMileageKm: integer('target_mileage_km'),
    99→    // mileage_variance_km is calculated: current - target (done in queries)
   100→
   101→    // Canvas display properties
   102→    displayColor: varchar('display_color', { length: 7 }),
   103→    displayOrder: integer('display_order'),
   104→
   105→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   106→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   107→  },
   108→  (table) => [
   109→    index('idx_trains_fleet').on(table.fleetId),
   110→    index('idx_trains_fitness').on(table.fitnessStatus),
   111→    index('idx_trains_depot').on(table.currentDepotId),
   112→  ]
   113→);
   114→
   115→/**
   116→ * Routes - Operating lines/services
   117→ *
   118→ * A route represents a service that trains operate on.
   119→ * Each route has expected daily mileage and train requirements.
   120→ *
   121→ * v2.1 additions:
   122→ * - signaling_system, power_system: For compatibility matching
   123→ * - tunnel_clearance_mm: Maximum vehicle width allowed
   124→ * - allowed_fleet_ids: Which fleets can operate on this route
   125→ */
   126→export const routes = pgTable(
   127→  'routes',
   128→  {
   129→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   130→    routeCode: varchar('route_code', { length: 20 }).notNull().unique(),
   131→    routeName: varchar('route_name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
   132→    lineId: varchar('line_id', { length: 20 }),
   133→    expectedKmPerDay: integer('expected_km_per_day').notNull(),
   134→    requiredTrains: integer('required_trains').notNull(),
   135→
   136→    // v2.1: Route characteristics for compatibility matching
   137→    defaultDailyKm: integer('default_daily_km'),
   138→    signalingSystem: varchar('signaling_system', { length: 30 }), // 'ATC', 'CBTC', 'ERTMS', 'LEGACY'
   139→    powerSystem: varchar('power_system', { length: 30 }), // 'THIRD_RAIL_750V', 'OVERHEAD_25KV', etc.
   140→    tunnelClearanceMm: integer('tunnel_clearance_mm'),
   141→    platformLengthM: decimal('platform_length_m', { precision: 5, scale: 2 }),
   142→    maxAxleLoadTonnes: decimal('max_axle_load_tonnes', { precision: 4, scale: 2 }),
   143→
   144→    // Restrictions - stored as array of UUID strings
   145→    allowedFleetIds: text('allowed_fleet_ids').array(),
   146→    requiresAto: boolean('requires_ato').default(false),
   147→    minCars: integer('min_cars'),
   148→    maxCars: integer('max_cars'),
   149→
   150→    active: boolean('active').default(true),
   151→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   152→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   153→  },
   154→  (table) => [uniqueIndex('idx_routes_code').on(table.routeCode)]
   155→);
   156→
   157→/**
   158→ * Tracks - Maintenance bays
   159→ *
   160→ * Physical maintenance positions within a depot.
   161→ * Each track has a type that determines what maintenance can be performed.
   162→ * Track capabilities (pit, crane, etc.) are stored in a separate table.
   163→ */
   164→export const tracks = pgTable(
   165→  'tracks',
   166→  {
   167→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   168→    depotId: uuid('depot_id')
   169→      .notNull()
   170→      .references(() => depots.id),
   171→    trackNumber: varchar('track_number', { length: 20 }).notNull(),
   172→    trackType: trackTypeEnum('track_type').notNull(),
   173→    maxTrainLengthM: decimal('max_train_length_m', { precision: 5, scale: 2 }),
   174→    hasOverheadCrane: boolean('has_overhead_crane').default(false),
   175→    hasPitAccess: boolean('has_pit_access').default(false),
   176→
   177→    active: boolean('active').default(true),
   178→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   179→  },
   180→  (table) => [
   181→    uniqueIndex('idx_tracks_depot_number').on(table.depotId, table.trackNumber),
   182→    index('idx_tracks_depot_type').on(table.depotId, table.trackType),
   183→  ]
   184→);
   185→
   186→// ============================================================================
   187→// Relations
   188→// ============================================================================
   189→
   190→export const depotsRelations = relations(depots, ({ many }) => ({
   191→  fleets: many(fleets),
   192→  trainsCurrentlyAt: many(trains, { relationName: 'currentDepot' }),
   193→  trainsHomeAt: many(trains, { relationName: 'homeDepot' }),
   194→  tracks: many(tracks),
   195→}));
   196→
   197→export const fleetsRelations = relations(fleets, ({ one, many }) => ({
   198→  baseDepot: one(depots, {
   199→    fields: [fleets.baseDepotId],
   200→    references: [depots.id],
   201→  }),
   202→  trains: many(trains),
   203→}));
   204→
   205→export const trainsRelations = relations(trains, ({ one }) => ({
   206→  fleet: one(fleets, {
   207→    fields: [trains.fleetId],
   208→    references: [fleets.id],
   209→  }),
   210→  currentDepot: one(depots, {
   211→    fields: [trains.currentDepotId],
   212→    references: [depots.id],
   213→    relationName: 'currentDepot',
   214→  }),
   215→  homeDepot: one(depots, {
   216→    fields: [trains.homeDepotId],
   217→    references: [depots.id],
   218→    relationName: 'homeDepot',
   219→  }),
   220→}));
   221→
   222→export const tracksRelations = relations(tracks, ({ one }) => ({
   223→  depot: one(depots, {
   224→    fields: [tracks.depotId],
   225→    references: [depots.id],
   226→  }),
   227→}));
   228→

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Operational Constraints Tables (v2.1)
     3→ *
     4→ * Critical tables for operational requirements and constraints:
     5→ * - Service demand: Minimum train availability (HARD constraint)
     6→ * - Route utilization profiles: Mileage projection accuracy
     7→ * - Unavailability events: CM/incident tracking (Maximo integration)
     8→ * - Train-route compatibility: Technical constraints
     9→ *
    10→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Sections 3.2.1 - 3.2.4
    11→ */
    12→
    13→import { relations, sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
    14→import {
    15→  boolean,
    16→  check,
    17→  date,
    18→  decimal,
    19→  index,
    20→  integer,
    21→  pgTable,
    22→  text,
    23→  timestamp,
    24→  uniqueIndex,
    25→  uuid,
    26→  varchar,
    27→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    28→import { fleets, routes, trains } from './core-fleet';
    29→import {
    30→  confidenceLevelEnum,
    31→  constraintTypeEnum,
    32→  dayTypeEnum,
    33→  fitnessStatusEnum,
    34→  unavailabilityStatusEnum,
    35→  unavailabilityTypeEnum,
    36→} from './enums';
    37→import { users } from './users';
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Service Demand - Minimum train availability requirements
    41→ *
    42→ * This is a HARD CONSTRAINT for the optimizer - it cannot schedule
    43→ * maintenance that would violate availability targets.
    44→ *
    45→ * Service demand varies by:
    46→ * - Fleet (different fleets serve different lines)
    47→ * - Day type (weekday vs. weekend vs. holiday)
    48→ * - Time of day (peak AM/PM vs. midday vs. evening)
    49→ */
    50→export const serviceDemand = pgTable(
    51→  'service_demand',
    52→  {
    53→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    54→    fleetId: uuid('fleet_id')
    55→      .notNull()
    56→      .references(() => fleets.id),
    57→    effectiveDate: date('effective_date').notNull(),
    58→    dayType: dayTypeEnum('day_type').notNull(),
    59→
    60→    // Minimum availability requirements
    61→    minTrainsRequired: integer('min_trains_required').notNull(),
    62→    peakAmRequirement: integer('peak_am_requirement'), // 6:00-9:00
    63→    middayRequirement: integer('midday_requirement'), // 9:00-16:00
    64→    peakPmRequirement: integer('peak_pm_requirement'), // 16:00-19:00
    65→    eveningRequirement: integer('evening_requirement'), // 19:00-23:00
    66→    standbyBuffer: integer('standby_buffer').default(2), // Additional contingency
    67→
    68→    // Metadata
    69→    source: varchar('source', { length: 50 }), // 'OPERATIONS', 'CONTRACT', 'MANUAL'
    70→    notes: text('notes'),
    71→
    72→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    73→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    74→    createdBy: uuid('created_by').references(() => users.id),
    75→  },
    76→  (table) => [
    77→    uniqueIndex('idx_service_demand_unique').on(table.fleetId, table.effectiveDate, table.dayType),
    78→    index('idx_service_demand_fleet').on(table.fleetId, table.effectiveDate),
    79→    index('idx_service_demand_lookup').on(table.fleetId, table.dayType, table.effectiveDate),
    80→  ]
    81→);
    82→
    83→/**
    84→ * Route Utilization Profiles - Expected daily mileage by route
    85→ *
    86→ * Enables accurate long-term mileage projections. Without route-specific
    87→ * profiles, projections use fleet-wide averages which can be off by
    88→ * 60,000+ km over a 12-year contract period.
    89→ *
    90→ * Key fields:
    91→ * - weekday_km, saturday_km, sunday_km: Daily mileage by day type
    92→ * - seasonal multipliers: Adjust for holiday periods
    93→ * - confidence: Data quality indicator
    94→ */
    95→export const routeUtilizationProfiles = pgTable(
    96→  'route_utilization_profiles',
    97→  {
    98→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    99→    routeId: uuid('route_id')
   100→      .notNull()
   101→      .references(() => routes.id),
   102→    effectiveDate: date('effective_date').notNull().default(sql`CURRENT_DATE`),
   103→
   104→    // Daily mileage by day type (km)
   105→    weekdayKm: integer('weekday_km').notNull(),
   106→    saturdayKm: integer('saturday_km'),
   107→    sundayKm: integer('sunday_km'),
   108→    holidayKm: integer('holiday_km'),
   109→
   110→    // Seasonal adjustments (e.g., 1.15 for CNY peak)
   111→    peakSeasonMultiplier: decimal('peak_season_multiplier', { precision: 3, scale: 2 }).default(
   112→      '1.0'
   113→    ),
   114→    offPeakMultiplier: decimal('off_peak_multiplier', { precision: 3, scale: 2 }).default('1.0'),
   115→
   116→    // Service characteristics
   117→    serviceHoursPerDay: decimal('service_hours_per_day', { precision: 4, scale: 1 }),
   118→    roundTripKm: decimal('round_trip_km', { precision: 6, scale: 2 }),
   119→    tripsPerDay: integer('trips_per_day'),
   120→
   121→    // Metadata
   122→    source: varchar('source', { length: 50 }), // 'MEASURED', 'ESTIMATED', 'CONTRACT'
   123→    confidence: confidenceLevelEnum('confidence').default('MEDIUM'),
   124→    notes: text('notes'),
   125→
   126→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   127→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   128→  },
   129→  (table) => [
   130→    uniqueIndex('idx_route_util_unique').on(table.routeId, table.effectiveDate),
   131→    index('idx_route_util_route').on(table.routeId, table.effectiveDate),
   132→  ]
   133→);
   134→
   135→/**
   136→ * Unavailability Events - Trains out of service
   137→ *
   138→ * CRITICAL integration point with Maximo CMMS. Tracks trains that cannot
   139→ * be scheduled due to corrective maintenance, incidents, or other factors.
   140→ *
   141→ * Key behaviors:
   142→ * - When an event is created with status='ACTIVE', the train's fitness_status
   143→ *   is automatically updated via trigger
   144→ * - Events with restriction_level='GROUNDED' block both run assignment and PM
   145→ * - Events with restriction_level='RESTRICTED' may allow limited operations
   146→ *
   147→ * Maximo integration:
   148→ * - source_system='MAXIMO' for CM work orders
   149→ * - external_reference holds the Maximo WO number (e.g., 'WO-2026-001234')
   150→ * - external_status mirrors Maximo status (WAPPR, INPRG, COMP, etc.)
   151→ */
   152→export const unavailabilityEvents = pgTable(
   153→  'unavailability_events',
   154→  {
   155→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   156→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
   157→      .notNull()
   158→      .references(() => trains.id),
   159→
   160→    // Event classification
   161→    eventType: unavailabilityTypeEnum('event_type').notNull(),
   162→    status: unavailabilityStatusEnum('status').notNull().default('ACTIVE'),
   163→    restrictionLevel: fitnessStatusEnum('restriction_level').notNull(), // 'RESTRICTED' or 'GROUNDED'
   164→
   165→    // Timing
   166→    reportedAt: timestamp('reported_at', { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
   167→    estimatedRepairHours: integer('estimated_repair_hours'),
   168→    estimatedReturnDate: date('estimated_return_date'),
   169→    actualReturnDate: date('actual_return_date'),
   170→
   171→    // External system linkage (Maximo integration)
   172→    sourceSystem: varchar('source_system', { length: 30 }), // 'MAXIMO', 'OCC', 'MANUAL'
   173→    externalReference: varchar('external_reference', { length: 50 }), // Maximo WO number
   174→    externalStatus: varchar('external_status', { length: 30 }), // Maximo status
   175→
   176→    // Description
   177→    description: text('description'),
   178→    failureCode: varchar('failure_code', { length: 30 }), // Standard failure classification
   179→    affectedSystem: varchar('affected_system', { length: 50 }), // 'TRACTION', 'BRAKES', etc.
   180→
   181→    // Scheduling impact flags
   182→    blocksPmScheduling: boolean('blocks_pm_scheduling').default(true),
   183→    blocksRunAssignment: boolean('blocks_run_assignment').default(true),
   184→
   185→    // Resolution
   186→    resolvedAt: timestamp('resolved_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   187→    resolvedBy: uuid('resolved_by').references(() => users.id),
   188→    resolutionNotes: text('resolution_notes'),
   189→
   190→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   191→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   192→  },
   193→  (table) => [
   194→    // Constraint: Cannot have resolved data if status is ACTIVE
   195→    check(
   196→      'chk_resolved_status',
   197→      sql`(${table.status} = 'ACTIVE' AND ${table.resolvedAt} IS NULL) OR (${table.status} = 'RESOLVED' AND ${table.resolvedAt} IS NOT NULL)`
   198→    ),
   199→    // Primary query: Find active unavailability for a train
   200→    index('idx_unavailability_active').on(table.trainId, table.status),
   201→    // Dashboard query: All active unavailability by estimated return
   202→    index('idx_unavailability_return').on(table.estimatedReturnDate),
   203→    // Maximo sync: Find by external reference
   204→    index('idx_unavailability_external').on(table.sourceSystem, table.externalReference),
   205→    // Audit: Find by type and date range
   206→    index('idx_unavailability_audit').on(table.eventType, table.reportedAt),
   207→  ]
   208→);
   209→
   210→/**
   211→ * Train-Route Compatibility - Technical constraints
   212→ *
   213→ * Defines which trains can operate on which routes based on technical
   214→ * constraints (signaling, gauge, power supply, etc.). Prevents the
   215→ * optimizer from assigning incompatible trains to routes.
   216→ *
   217→ * Can be defined at:
   218→ * - Fleet level (fleet_id): Applies to all trains in the fleet
   219→ * - Train level (train_id): Overrides fleet-level for specific train
   220→ *
   221→ * Temporal support:
   222→ * - effective_date/expiry_date allow temporary restrictions
   223→ * - NULL expiry_date = permanent restriction
   224→ */
   225→export const trainRouteCompatibility = pgTable(
   226→  'train_route_compatibility',
   227→  {
   228→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   229→
   230→    // Can be at train or fleet level (mutually exclusive)
   231→    trainId: uuid('train_id').references(() => trains.id),
   232→    fleetId: uuid('fleet_id').references(() => fleets.id),
   233→    routeId: uuid('route_id')
   234→      .notNull()
   235→      .references(() => routes.id),
   236→
   237→    // Compatibility determination
   238→    isCompatible: boolean('is_compatible').notNull().default(true),
   239→    constraintType: constraintTypeEnum('constraint_type'),
   240→    restrictionReason: text('restriction_reason'),
   241→
   242→    // Validity period (for temporary restrictions)
   243→    effectiveDate: date('effective_date').default(sql`CURRENT_DATE`),
   244→    expiryDate: date('expiry_date'), // NULL = permanent
   245→
   246→    // Metadata
   247→    verifiedBy: uuid('verified_by').references(() => users.id),
   248→    verifiedAt: timestamp('verified_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   249→    notes: text('notes'),
   250→
   251→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   252→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   253→  },
   254→  (table) => [
   255→    // Must specify either train_id OR fleet_id, not both
   256→    check(
   257→      'chk_train_or_fleet',
   258→      sql`(${table.trainId} IS NOT NULL AND ${table.fleetId} IS NULL) OR (${table.trainId} IS NULL AND ${table.fleetId} IS NOT NULL)`
   259→    ),
   260→    index('idx_compatibility_route').on(table.routeId, table.isCompatible),
   261→    index('idx_compatibility_train').on(table.trainId, table.isCompatible),
   262→    index('idx_compatibility_fleet').on(table.fleetId, table.isCompatible),
   263→    index('idx_compatibility_active').on(table.effectiveDate, table.expiryDate),
   264→  ]
   265→);
   266→
   267→// ============================================================================
   268→// Relations
   269→// ============================================================================
   270→
   271→export const serviceDemandRelations = relations(serviceDemand, ({ one }) => ({
   272→  fleet: one(fleets, {
   273→    fields: [serviceDemand.fleetId],
   274→    references: [fleets.id],
   275→  }),
   276→  createdByUser: one(users, {
   277→    fields: [serviceDemand.createdBy],
   278→    references: [users.id],
   279→  }),
   280→}));
   281→
   282→export const routeUtilizationProfilesRelations = relations(routeUtilizationProfiles, ({ one }) => ({
   283→  route: one(routes, {
   284→    fields: [routeUtilizationProfiles.routeId],
   285→    references: [routes.id],
   286→  }),
   287→}));
   288→
   289→export const unavailabilityEventsRelations = relations(unavailabilityEvents, ({ one }) => ({
   290→  train: one(trains, {
   291→    fields: [unavailabilityEvents.trainId],
   292→    references: [trains.id],
   293→  }),
   294→  resolvedByUser: one(users, {
   295→    fields: [unavailabilityEvents.resolvedBy],
   296→    references: [users.id],
   297→  }),
   298→}));
   299→
   300→export const trainRouteCompatibilityRelations = relations(trainRouteCompatibility, ({ one }) => ({
   301→  train: one(trains, {
   302→    fields: [trainRouteCompatibility.trainId],
   303→    references: [trains.id],
   304→  }),
   305→  fleet: one(fleets, {
   306→    fields: [trainRouteCompatibility.fleetId],
   307→    references: [fleets.id],
   308→  }),
   309→  route: one(routes, {
   310→    fields: [trainRouteCompatibility.routeId],
   311→    references: [routes.id],
   312→  }),
   313→  verifiedByUser: one(users, {
   314→    fields: [trainRouteCompatibility.verifiedBy],
   315→    references: [users.id],
   316→  }),
   317→}));
   318→

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Track Capabilities Tables (v2.1)
     3→ *
     4→ * Track equipment/features and PM requirements for constraint-based scheduling.
     5→ * Also includes PM baseline assumptions for cold start data migration.
     6→ *
     7→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Sections 3.2.5 - 3.2.6
     8→ */
     9→
    10→import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    11→import {
    12→  boolean,
    13→  date,
    14→  index,
    15→  integer,
    16→  jsonb,
    17→  pgTable,
    18→  text,
    19→  timestamp,
    20→  uniqueIndex,
    21→  uuid,
    22→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    23→import { tracks, trains } from './core-fleet';
    24→import {
    25→  assumptionTypeEnum,
    26→  confidenceLevelEnum,
    27→  trackCapabilityEnum,
    28→} from './enums';
    29→import { pmTypes } from './pm-management';
    30→import { users } from './users';
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * Track Capabilities - Equipment and features per track
    34→ *
    35→ * Defines specific equipment available on each maintenance track.
    36→ * Used to match PM requirements to capable tracks.
    37→ *
    38→ * Examples:
    39→ * - Track T1 at Bishan has: PIT, CRANE, LIFTING_JACKS
    40→ * - Track T5 at Changi has: WHEEL_LATHE, DROP_TABLE
    41→ */
    42→export const trackCapabilities = pgTable(
    43→  'track_capabilities',
    44→  {
    45→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    46→    trackId: uuid('track_id')
    47→      .notNull()
    48→      .references(() => tracks.id),
    49→    capability: trackCapabilityEnum('capability').notNull(),
    50→
    51→    // Capacity limits for this capability (e.g., max lift weight)
    52→    capacityLimit: jsonb('capacity_limit'), // {"max_axle_load_tonnes": 17, "max_lift_tonnes": 25}
    53→
    54→    // Availability
    55→    isOperational: boolean('is_operational').default(true),
    56→    maintenanceSchedule: text('maintenance_schedule'), // "Unavailable Tuesdays 06:00-10:00"
    57→
    58→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    59→  },
    60→  (table) => [
    61→    uniqueIndex('idx_track_cap_unique').on(table.trackId, table.capability),
    62→    index('idx_track_cap_track').on(table.trackId),
    63→    index('idx_track_cap_type').on(table.capability),
    64→  ]
    65→);
    66→
    67→/**
    68→ * PM Capability Requirements - What capabilities each PM type needs
    69→ *
    70→ * Links PM types to required track capabilities.
    71→ * Mandatory vs. preferred:
    72→ * - is_mandatory=true: PM cannot proceed without this capability
    73→ * - is_mandatory=false: Preferred but can use alternative
    74→ *
    75→ * Example: C4 overhaul requires PIT (mandatory), LIFTING_JACKS (mandatory), CRANE (preferred)
    76→ */
    77→export const pmCapabilityRequirements = pgTable(
    78→  'pm_capability_requirements',
    79→  {
    80→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    81→    pmTypeId: uuid('pm_type_id')
    82→      .notNull()
    83→      .references(() => pmTypes.id),
    84→    capability: trackCapabilityEnum('capability').notNull(),
    85→    isMandatory: boolean('is_mandatory').default(true),
    86→
    87→    notes: text('notes'), // Why this capability is needed
    88→
    89→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    90→  },
    91→  (table) => [
    92→    uniqueIndex('idx_pm_cap_req_unique').on(table.pmTypeId, table.capability),
    93→    index('idx_pm_cap_req_pm').on(table.pmTypeId),
    94→    index('idx_pm_cap_req_cap').on(table.capability),
    95→  ]
    96→);
    97→
    98→/**
    99→ * PM Baseline Assumptions - Cold start data migration
   100→ *
   101→ * Handles scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration.
   102→ * Tracks how PM dates/mileage were inferred with confidence levels.
   103→ *
   104→ * Inference rules (implemented in application logic):
   105→ * 1. INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING: If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval
   106→ *    is 6 months, assume first PM at 2020-07-01
   107→ * 2. INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM: If C4 completed 2024-06-15, C1/C2/C3 counters reset
   108→ *    from that date
   109→ * 3. NO_HISTORY: No data, use commissioning date with requires_review=true
   110→ *
   111→ * Records with LOW/ESTIMATED confidence should have requires_review=true
   112→ * for manual verification by operators.
   113→ */
   114→export const pmBaselineAssumptions = pgTable(
   115→  'pm_baseline_assumptions',
   116→  {
   117→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   118→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
   119→      .notNull()
   120→      .references(() => trains.id),
   121→    pmTypeId: uuid('pm_type_id')
   122→      .notNull()
   123→      .references(() => pmTypes.id),
   124→
   125→    // Assumption details
   126→    assumptionType: assumptionTypeEnum('assumption_type').notNull(),
   127→
   128→    // Assumed baseline values
   129→    assumedLastPmDate: date('assumed_last_pm_date'),
   130→    assumedLastPmMileage: integer('assumed_last_pm_mileage'),
   131→    confidenceLevel: confidenceLevelEnum('confidence_level').notNull(),
   132→
   133→    // Inference source documentation
   134→    inferenceSource: text('inference_source'), // How assumption was derived
   135→    inferenceData: jsonb('inference_data'), // Supporting data
   136→
   137→    // Review workflow
   138→    requiresReview: boolean('requires_review').default(false),
   139→    reviewedBy: uuid('reviewed_by').references(() => users.id),
   140→    reviewedAt: timestamp('reviewed_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   141→    reviewNotes: text('review_notes'),
   142→
   143→    // When applied to the system
   144→    appliedAt: timestamp('applied_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   145→    appliedBy: uuid('applied_by').references(() => users.id),
   146→
   147→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   148→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   149→  },
   150→  (table) => [
   151→    uniqueIndex('idx_assumptions_unique').on(table.trainId, table.pmTypeId),
   152→    index('idx_assumptions_train').on(table.trainId),
   153→    index('idx_assumptions_review').on(table.requiresReview),
   154→    index('idx_assumptions_confidence').on(table.confidenceLevel),
   155→  ]
   156→);
   157→
   158→// ============================================================================
   159→// Relations
   160→// ============================================================================
   161→
   162→export const trackCapabilitiesRelations = relations(trackCapabilities, ({ one }) => ({
   163→  track: one(tracks, {
   164→    fields: [trackCapabilities.trackId],
   165→    references: [tracks.id],
   166→  }),
   167→}));
   168→
   169→export const pmCapabilityRequirementsRelations = relations(pmCapabilityRequirements, ({ one }) => ({
   170→  pmType: one(pmTypes, {
   171→    fields: [pmCapabilityRequirements.pmTypeId],
   172→    references: [pmTypes.id],
   173→  }),
   174→}));
   175→
   176→export const pmBaselineAssumptionsRelations = relations(pmBaselineAssumptions, ({ one }) => ({
   177→  train: one(trains, {
   178→    fields: [pmBaselineAssumptions.trainId],
   179→    references: [trains.id],
   180→  }),
   181→  pmType: one(pmTypes, {
   182→    fields: [pmBaselineAssumptions.pmTypeId],
   183→    references: [pmTypes.id],
   184→  }),
   185→  reviewedByUser: one(users, {
   186→    fields: [pmBaselineAssumptions.reviewedBy],
   187→    references: [users.id],
   188→    relationName: 'reviewedBy',
   189→  }),
   190→  appliedByUser: one(users, {
   191→    fields: [pmBaselineAssumptions.appliedBy],
   192→    references: [users.id],
   193→    relationName: 'appliedBy',
   194→  }),
   195→}));
   196→

1→/**
     2→ * MMS PM Management Tables
     3→ *
     4→ * Preventive maintenance scheduling: PM types, material requirements, and schedules.
     5→ * Core tables for the maintenance planning workflow.
     6→ *
     7→ * PM Hierarchy: Higher-level PMs satisfy lower-level requirements
     8→ * - C4 satisfies C1, C2, C3 (resets their counters)
     9→ * - Overhaul satisfies all PM types
    10→ *
    11→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Sections 3.5
    12→ */
    13→
    14→import { relations, sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
    15→import {
    16→  boolean,
    17→  date,
    18→  decimal,
    19→  index,
    20→  integer,
    21→  pgTable,
    22→  text,
    23→  timestamp,
    24→  uniqueIndex,
    25→  uuid,
    26→  varchar,
    27→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    28→import { depots, fleets, tracks, trains } from './core-fleet';
    29→import { materialCriticalityEnum, pmStatusEnum, shiftTypeEnum, trackTypeEnum } from './enums';
    30→import { users } from './users';
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * PM Types - Maintenance task definitions
    34→ *
    35→ * Defines the maintenance tasks for each fleet with their intervals
    36→ * and resource requirements.
    37→ *
    38→ * Key concepts:
    39→ * - interval_km / interval_months: Whichever triggers first
    40→ * - satisfies_codes: PM hierarchy (C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3)
    41→ * - early/late tolerance: Compliance window (default ±10%)
    42→ *
    43→ * Version management: PM intervals can change over the 12-year contract.
    44→ * The effective_from/effective_to fields support versioning.
    45→ */
    46→export const pmTypes = pgTable(
    47→  'pm_types',
    48→  {
    49→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    50→    fleetId: uuid('fleet_id')
    51→      .notNull()
    52→      .references(() => fleets.id),
    53→    pmCode: varchar('pm_code', { length: 20 }).notNull(), // 'C1', 'C2', 'C3', 'C4', 'OVERHAUL'
    54→    pmName: varchar('pm_name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    55→    version: integer('version').default(1),
    56→
    57→    // Interval thresholds (whichever comes first)
    58→    intervalKm: integer('interval_km'),
    59→    intervalMonths: integer('interval_months'),
    60→
    61→    // PM Hierarchy: which lower-level PMs does this PM satisfy?
    62→    // When a C4 is completed, it satisfies C1/C2/C3 requirements
    63→    // Example: C4 row has satisfies_codes = ['C1', 'C2', 'C3']
    64→    satisfiesCodes: text('satisfies_codes').array(),
    65→
    66→    // Resource requirements
    67→    estimatedHours: decimal('estimated_hours', { precision: 6, scale: 2 }).notNull(),
    68→    trackType: trackTypeEnum('track_type').notNull(),
    69→    minTrackDays: integer('min_track_days').default(1), // How many days track is occupied
    70→
    71→    // Compliance window tolerance (percentage)
    72→    earlyTolerancePct: decimal('early_tolerance_pct', { precision: 5, scale: 2 }).default('10.0'),
    73→    lateTolerancePct: decimal('late_tolerance_pct', { precision: 5, scale: 2 }).default('10.0'),
    74→
    75→    // Certification requirements
    76→    requiredCertifications: text('required_certifications').array(),
    77→
    78→    // v2.1: PM type versioning for interval changes over contract life
    79→    effectiveFrom: date('effective_from').notNull().default(sql`CURRENT_DATE`),
    80→    effectiveTo: date('effective_to'), // NULL = current version
    81→    supersededBy: uuid('superseded_by'), // Link to newer version
    82→
    83→    // Canvas display properties
    84→    displayColor: varchar('display_color', { length: 7 }),
    85→    displayPriority: integer('display_priority').default(50),
    86→
    87→    active: boolean('active').default(true),
    88→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    89→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    90→  },
    91→  (table) => [
    92→    uniqueIndex('idx_pm_types_unique').on(table.fleetId, table.pmCode, table.version),
    93→    index('idx_pm_types_current').on(table.fleetId, table.pmCode, table.effectiveFrom),
    94→  ]
    95→);
    96→
    97→/**
    98→ * PM Material Requirements - Bill of Materials
    99→ *
   100→ * Links PM types to required parts with quantities and criticality.
   101→ *
   102→ * Criticality levels determine scheduling constraints:
   103→ * - BLOCKING: Cannot start PM without part (14-day hard constraint)
   104→ * - DEFERRABLE: Can start, must have within X days
   105→ * - CONSUMABLE: Nice-to-have, can substitute
   106→ */
   107→export const pmMaterialRequirements = pgTable(
   108→  'pm_material_requirements',
   109→  {
   110→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   111→    pmTypeId: uuid('pm_type_id')
   112→      .notNull()
   113→      .references(() => pmTypes.id),
   114→    partNumber: varchar('part_number', { length: 50 }).notNull(),
   115→    partDescription: text('part_description'),
   116→
   117→    quantityPerPm: integer('quantity_per_pm').notNull().default(1),
   118→    criticality: materialCriticalityEnum('criticality').notNull().default('BLOCKING'),
   119→
   120→    leadTimeDays: integer('lead_time_days').default(14),
   121→    substituteParts: text('substitute_parts').array(), // Alternative part numbers
   122→
   123→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   124→  },
   125→  (table) => [
   126→    uniqueIndex('idx_pm_materials_unique').on(table.pmTypeId, table.partNumber),
   127→    index('idx_pm_materials_type').on(table.pmTypeId),
   128→  ]
   129→);
   130→
   131→/**
   132→ * PM Schedule - Planned and executed maintenance
   133→ *
   134→ * The core scheduling table. Each row represents a planned or completed PM.
   135→ *
   136→ * Key fields:
   137→ * - constraint_start/end: Compliance window (calculated from PM type tolerances)
   138→ * - is_locked: When true, optimizer cannot move this PM
   139→ * - maximo_wo_id: Link to Maximo work order (when INPRG, PM is locked)
   140→ * - scenario_id: NULL for production, non-NULL for what-if scenarios
   141→ *
   142→ * Status transitions:
   143→ * SCHEDULED -> IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED
   144→ * SCHEDULED -> DEFERRED -> SCHEDULED (new date)
   145→ * SCHEDULED -> CANCELLED
   146→ */
   147→export const pmSchedule = pgTable(
   148→  'pm_schedule',
   149→  {
   150→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   151→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
   152→      .notNull()
   153→      .references(() => trains.id),
   154→    pmTypeId: uuid('pm_type_id')
   155→      .notNull()
   156→      .references(() => pmTypes.id),
   157→
   158→    // Scheduling
   159→    targetDate: date('target_date').notNull(),
   160→    targetMileageKm: integer('target_mileage_km'),
   161→    status: pmStatusEnum('status').default('SCHEDULED'),
   162→
   163→    // Compliance window
   164→    constraintStart: date('constraint_start').notNull(), // Earliest allowed date
   165→    constraintEnd: date('constraint_end').notNull(), // Latest allowed date
   166→
   167→    // Resource assignment
   168→    assignedDepotId: uuid('assigned_depot_id').references(() => depots.id),
   169→    assignedTrackId: uuid('assigned_track_id').references(() => tracks.id),
   170→    assignedHours: decimal('assigned_hours', { precision: 6, scale: 2 }),
   171→    assignedShift: shiftTypeEnum('assigned_shift'),
   172→
   173→    // Execution
   174→    actualStartDate: date('actual_start_date'),
   175→    actualEndDate: date('actual_end_date'),
   176→    actualHours: decimal('actual_hours', { precision: 6, scale: 2 }),
   177→    completedAt: timestamp('completed_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   178→    completedMileageKm: integer('completed_mileage_km'),
   179→
   180→    // Work order integration (Maximo)
   181→    maximoWoId: varchar('maximo_wo_id', { length: 50 }),
   182→
   183→    // Locking - prevents optimizer from moving
   184→    isLocked: boolean('is_locked').default(false),
   185→    lockedReason: varchar('locked_reason', { length: 100 }),
   186→    lockedBy: uuid('locked_by').references(() => users.id),
   187→    lockedAt: timestamp('locked_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   188→
   189→    // Scenario support (NULL = production schedule)
   190→    scenarioId: uuid('scenario_id'),
   191→
   192→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   193→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   194→    createdBy: uuid('created_by').references(() => users.id),
   195→  },
   196→  (table) => [
   197→    index('idx_pm_schedule_train').on(table.trainId, table.targetDate),
   198→    index('idx_pm_schedule_date').on(table.targetDate, table.status),
   199→    index('idx_pm_schedule_depot').on(table.assignedDepotId, table.targetDate),
   200→    index('idx_pm_schedule_scenario').on(table.scenarioId),
   201→    index('idx_pm_schedule_canvas').on(table.targetDate, table.trainId),
   202→    index('idx_pm_schedule_maximo').on(table.maximoWoId),
   203→  ]
   204→);
   205→
   206→// ============================================================================
   207→// Relations
   208→// ============================================================================
   209→
   210→export const pmTypesRelations = relations(pmTypes, ({ one, many }) => ({
   211→  fleet: one(fleets, {
   212→    fields: [pmTypes.fleetId],
   213→    references: [fleets.id],
   214→  }),
   215→  materialRequirements: many(pmMaterialRequirements),
   216→  schedules: many(pmSchedule),
   217→}));
   218→
   219→export const pmMaterialRequirementsRelations = relations(pmMaterialRequirements, ({ one }) => ({
   220→  pmType: one(pmTypes, {
   221→    fields: [pmMaterialRequirements.pmTypeId],
   222→    references: [pmTypes.id],
   223→  }),
   224→}));
   225→
   226→export const pmScheduleRelations = relations(pmSchedule, ({ one }) => ({
   227→  train: one(trains, {
   228→    fields: [pmSchedule.trainId],
   229→    references: [trains.id],
   230→  }),
   231→  pmType: one(pmTypes, {
   232→    fields: [pmSchedule.pmTypeId],
   233→    references: [pmTypes.id],
   234→  }),
   235→  assignedDepot: one(depots, {
   236→    fields: [pmSchedule.assignedDepotId],
   237→    references: [depots.id],
   238→  }),
   239→  assignedTrack: one(tracks, {
   240→    fields: [pmSchedule.assignedTrackId],
   241→    references: [tracks.id],
   242→  }),
   243→  lockedByUser: one(users, {
   244→    fields: [pmSchedule.lockedBy],
   245→    references: [users.id],
   246→    relationName: 'lockedBy',
   247→  }),
   248→  createdByUser: one(users, {

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Mileage Tracking Tables
     3→ *
     4→ * Time-series odometer data and meter events.
     5→ * Uses TimescaleDB hypertable for efficient storage and querying.
     6→ *
     7→ * Data flow:
     8→ * 1. CSV file arrives via file watcher
     9→ * 2. Ingestion worker validates and inserts into mileage_readings
    10→ * 3. Anomaly detection flags suspicious readings
    11→ * 4. Meter events track odometer replacements/resets
    12→ *
    13→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.3
    14→ */
    15→
    16→import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    17→import {
    18→  date,
    19→  index,
    20→  integer,
    21→  pgTable,
    22→  primaryKey,
    23→  text,
    24→  timestamp,
    25→  uuid,
    26→  varchar,
    27→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    28→import { trains } from './core-fleet';
    29→import { anomalyTypeEnum, meterEventTypeEnum, validationStatusEnum } from './enums';
    30→import { users } from './users';
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * Meter Events - Odometer replacements, resets, calibrations
    34→ *
    35→ * Tracks events that affect mileage continuity:
    36→ * - REPLACEMENT: New meter installed
    37→ * - RESET: Meter zeroed
    38→ * - CALIBRATION: Meter adjusted
    39→ * - SWAP: Meter moved between trains
    40→ *
    41→ * The adjustment_km field represents the cumulative mileage that must be
    42→ * added to all subsequent readings to maintain accurate lifetime mileage.
    43→ */
    44→export const meterEvents = pgTable(
    45→  'meter_events',
    46→  {
    47→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    48→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
    49→      .notNull()
    50→      .references(() => trains.id),
    51→    eventDate: date('event_date').notNull(),
    52→    eventType: meterEventTypeEnum('event_type').notNull(),
    53→
    54→    oldMeterSerial: varchar('old_meter_serial', { length: 50 }),
    55→    newMeterSerial: varchar('new_meter_serial', { length: 50 }),
    56→    oldMeterReading: integer('old_meter_reading'),
    57→    newMeterReading: integer('new_meter_reading'),
    58→    adjustmentKm: integer('adjustment_km'), // Cumulative adjustment to apply
    59→
    60→    reason: text('reason'),
    61→    approvedBy: uuid('approved_by').references(() => users.id),
    62→    approvedAt: timestamp('approved_at', { withTimezone: true }),
    63→
    64→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    65→    createdBy: uuid('created_by').references(() => users.id),
    66→  },
    67→  (table) => [index('idx_meter_events_train').on(table.trainId, table.eventDate)]
    68→);
    69→
    70→/**
    71→ * Mileage Readings - Daily odometer readings
    72→ *
    73→ * This table is designed for TimescaleDB hypertable conversion:
    74→ * - Partitioned by reading_date (7-day chunks)
    75→ * - Compressed after 30 days
    76→ * - Retained for 13 years (contract + buffer)
    77→ *
    78→ * Key fields:
    79→ * - raw_reading: Direct value from CSV
    80→ * - adjusted_reading: After meter event adjustments
    81→ * - daily_km: Calculated as today - yesterday
    82→ * - validation_status: PENDING -> VALID | ANOMALY -> CORRECTED
    83→ *
    84→ * Note: In production, this table should be converted to a TimescaleDB
    85→ * hypertable with:
    86→ * SELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'reading_date');
    87→ */
    88→export const mileageReadings = pgTable(
    89→  'mileage_readings',
    90→  {
    91→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
    92→      .notNull()
    93→      .references(() => trains.id),
    94→    readingDate: date('reading_date').notNull(),
    95→
    96→    // Raw and adjusted values
    97→    rawReading: integer('raw_reading').notNull(), // Direct from CSV
    98→    adjustedReading: integer('adjusted_reading').notNull(), // After meter adjustments
    99→    dailyKm: integer('daily_km'), // Calculated: today - yesterday
   100→
   101→    // Validation
   102→    validationStatus: validationStatusEnum('validation_status').default('PENDING'),
   103→    anomalyType: anomalyTypeEnum('anomaly_type'),
   104→    anomalyNotes: text('anomaly_notes'),
   105→
   106→    // Source tracking
   107→    sourceFile: varchar('source_file', { length: 255 }),
   108→    meterEventId: uuid('meter_event_id').references(() => meterEvents.id),
   109→
   110→    ingestedAt: timestamp('ingested_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   111→  },
   112→  (table) => [
   113→    primaryKey({ columns: [table.trainId, table.readingDate] }),
   114→    index('idx_mileage_train_date').on(table.trainId, table.readingDate),
   115→    index('idx_mileage_validation').on(table.validationStatus, table.readingDate),
   116→  ]
   117→);
   118→
   119→// ============================================================================
   120→// Relations
   121→// ============================================================================
   122→
   123→export const meterEventsRelations = relations(meterEvents, ({ one, many }) => ({
   124→  train: one(trains, {
   125→    fields: [meterEvents.trainId],
   126→    references: [trains.id],
   127→  }),
   128→  approvedByUser: one(users, {
   129→    fields: [meterEvents.approvedBy],
   130→    references: [users.id],
   131→    relationName: 'approvedBy',
   132→  }),
   133→  createdByUser: one(users, {
   134→    fields: [meterEvents.createdBy],
   135→    references: [users.id],
   136→    relationName: 'createdBy',
   137→  }),
   138→  mileageReadings: many(mileageReadings),
   139→}));
   140→
   141→export const mileageReadingsRelations = relations(mileageReadings, ({ one }) => ({
   142→  train: one(trains, {
   143→    fields: [mileageReadings.trainId],
   144→    references: [trains.id],
   145→  }),
   146→  meterEvent: one(meterEvents, {
   147→    fields: [mileageReadings.meterEventId],
   148→    references: [meterEvents.id],
   149→  }),
   150→}));
   151→

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Component Tracking Tables
     3→ *
     4→ * Tracks individual train components (bogies, motors, pantographs, etc.)
     5→ * through their lifecycle including installations and PM history.
     6→ *
     7→ * Key concept: When a component is swapped between trains, PM thresholds
     8→ * follow the component, not the train. This ensures accurate maintenance
     9→ * scheduling based on actual component usage.
    10→ *
    11→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.4
    12→ */
    13→
    14→import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    15→import {
    16→  date,
    17→  index,
    18→  integer,
    19→  pgTable,
    20→  timestamp,
    21→  uniqueIndex,
    22→  uuid,
    23→  varchar,
    24→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    25→import { trains } from './core-fleet';
    26→import { componentStatusEnum, removalReasonEnum } from './enums';
    27→import { pmTypes } from './pm-management';
    28→
    29→/**
    30→ * Train Components - Individual trackable parts
    31→ *
    32→ * Components that have their own mileage tracking and PM requirements.
    33→ * Examples: bogies, traction motors, pantographs, brake units.
    34→ *
    35→ * Key fields:
    36→ * - component_mileage_km: Cumulative mileage on this specific component
    37→ * - condition_score: 1-100 rating for predictive maintenance
    38→ */
    39→export const trainComponents = pgTable(
    40→  'train_components',
    41→  {
    42→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    43→    componentType: varchar('component_type', { length: 50 }).notNull(), // 'BOGIE', 'TRACTION_MOTOR', etc.
    44→    serialNumber: varchar('serial_number', { length: 100 }).notNull().unique(),
    45→    manufacturer: varchar('manufacturer', { length: 100 }),
    46→    model: varchar('model', { length: 100 }),
    47→
    48→    // Current installation
    49→    currentTrainId: uuid('current_train_id').references(() => trains.id),
    50→    currentPosition: varchar('current_position', { length: 50 }), // 'CAR_1_BOGIE_A', 'CAR_3_MOTOR_L'
    51→    installedDate: date('installed_date'),
    52→    installedMileageKm: integer('installed_mileage_km'), // Train mileage at installation
    53→
    54→    // Component lifecycle
    55→    commissioningDate: date('commissioning_date'),
    56→    componentMileageKm: integer('component_mileage_km').notNull().default(0),
    57→    lastOverhaulDate: date('last_overhaul_date'),
    58→    lastOverhaulMileageKm: integer('last_overhaul_mileage_km'),
    59→
    60→    // Status
    61→    status: componentStatusEnum('status').default('IN_SERVICE'),
    62→    conditionScore: integer('condition_score'), // 1-100
    63→
    64→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    65→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    66→  },
    67→  (table) => [
    68→    index('idx_components_train').on(table.currentTrainId),
    69→    index('idx_components_type').on(table.componentType, table.status),
    70→    uniqueIndex('idx_components_serial').on(table.serialNumber),
    71→  ]
    72→);
    73→
    74→/**
    75→ * Component Installations - Installation history
    76→ *
    77→ * Tracks the movement of components between trains.
    78→ * Used to calculate component-specific mileage accumulation.
    79→ */
    80→export const componentInstallations = pgTable(
    81→  'component_installations',
    82→  {
    83→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    84→    componentId: uuid('component_id')
    85→      .notNull()
    86→      .references(() => trainComponents.id),
    87→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
    88→      .notNull()
    89→      .references(() => trains.id),
    90→    position: varchar('position', { length: 50 }),
    91→
    92→    // Installation data
    93→    installedDate: date('installed_date').notNull(),
    94→    installedTrainMileageKm: integer('installed_train_mileage_km').notNull(),
    95→    installedComponentMileageKm: integer('installed_component_mileage_km').notNull(),
    96→
    97→    // Removal data (NULL if still installed)
    98→    removedDate: date('removed_date'),
    99→    removedTrainMileageKm: integer('removed_train_mileage_km'),
   100→    removedComponentMileageKm: integer('removed_component_mileage_km'),
   101→
   102→    removalReason: removalReasonEnum('removal_reason'),
   103→    workOrderId: varchar('work_order_id', { length: 50 }), // Link to Maximo
   104→
   105→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   106→  },
   107→  (table) => [
   108→    index('idx_installations_component').on(table.componentId, table.installedDate),
   109→    index('idx_installations_train').on(table.trainId, table.installedDate),
   110→  ]
   111→);
   112→
   113→/**
   114→ * Component PM Tracking - Component-level PM history
   115→ *
   116→ * PM thresholds for components that move between trains.
   117→ * When a component is swapped, its PM schedule stays with the component.
   118→ */
   119→export const componentPmTracking = pgTable(
   120→  'component_pm_tracking',
   121→  {
   122→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   123→    componentId: uuid('component_id')
   124→      .notNull()
   125→      .references(() => trainComponents.id),
   126→    pmTypeId: uuid('pm_type_id')
   127→      .notNull()
   128→      .references(() => pmTypes.id),
   129→
   130→    lastPmDate: date('last_pm_date'),
   131→    lastPmMileageKm: integer('last_pm_mileage_km'),
   132→    nextPmDueMileageKm: integer('next_pm_due_mileage_km'),
   133→    nextPmDueDate: date('next_pm_due_date'),
   134→
   135→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   136→  },
   137→  (table) => [
   138→    uniqueIndex('idx_component_pm_unique').on(table.componentId, table.pmTypeId),
   139→    index('idx_component_pm_component').on(table.componentId),
   140→  ]
   141→);
   142→
   143→// ============================================================================
   144→// Relations
   145→// ============================================================================
   146→
   147→export const trainComponentsRelations = relations(trainComponents, ({ one, many }) => ({
   148→  currentTrain: one(trains, {
   149→    fields: [trainComponents.currentTrainId],
   150→    references: [trains.id],
   151→  }),
   152→  installations: many(componentInstallations),
   153→  pmTracking: many(componentPmTracking),
   154→}));
   155→
   156→export const componentInstallationsRelations = relations(componentInstallations, ({ one }) => ({
   157→  component: one(trainComponents, {
   158→    fields: [componentInstallations.componentId],
   159→    references: [trainComponents.id],
   160→  }),
   161→  train: one(trains, {
   162→    fields: [componentInstallations.trainId],
   163→    references: [trains.id],
   164→  }),
   165→}));
   166→
   167→export const componentPmTrackingRelations = relations(componentPmTracking, ({ one }) => ({
   168→  component: one(trainComponents, {
   169→    fields: [componentPmTracking.componentId],
   170→    references: [trainComponents.id],
   171→  }),
   172→  pmType: one(pmTypes, {
   173→    fields: [componentPmTracking.pmTypeId],
   174→    references: [pmTypes.id],
   175→  }),
   176→}));

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Material Management Tables
     3→ *
     4→ * Inventory tracking and material reservations for PM scheduling.
     5→ * Materials are a constraint for PM scheduling - PMs cannot proceed
     6→ * without required blocking parts.
     7→ *
     8→ * Integration:
     9→ * - Inventory snapshots come from Maximo/ERP daily
    10→ * - Reservations are created when PMs are scheduled
    11→ * - Soft reservations auto-expire if PM moves
    12→ *
    13→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.6
    14→ */
    15→
    16→import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    17→import {
    18→  date,
    19→  decimal,
    20→  index,
    21→  integer,
    22→  pgTable,
    23→  text,
    24→  timestamp,
    25→  uniqueIndex,
    26→  uuid,
    27→  varchar,
    28→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    29→import { depots } from './core-fleet';
    30→import { reservationTypeEnum } from './enums';
    31→import { pmSchedule } from './pm-management';
    32→import { users } from './users';
    33→
    34→/**
    35→ * Inventory Positions - Current stock levels
    36→ *
    37→ * Snapshot of inventory from ERP/Maximo system.
    38→ * Updated daily via integration sync.
    39→ *
    40→ * Key fields:
    41→ * - quantity_available: Calculated as on_hand - reserved
    42→ * - reorder_point: Triggers replenishment when available drops below
    43→ */
    44→export const inventoryPositions = pgTable(
    45→  'inventory_positions',
    46→  {
    47→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    48→    depotId: uuid('depot_id')
    49→      .notNull()
    50→      .references(() => depots.id),
    51→    partNumber: varchar('part_number', { length: 50 }).notNull(),
    52→    partDescription: text('part_description'),
    53→
    54→    // Quantities
    55→    quantityOnHand: integer('quantity_on_hand').notNull().default(0),
    56→    quantityReserved: integer('quantity_reserved').notNull().default(0),
    57→    quantityOnOrder: integer('quantity_on_order').default(0),
    58→    // quantity_available is calculated in queries: on_hand - reserved
    59→
    60→    // Replenishment
    61→    reorderPoint: integer('reorder_point'),
    62→    reorderQuantity: integer('reorder_quantity'),
    63→    nextDeliveryDate: date('next_delivery_date'),
    64→    nextDeliveryQuantity: integer('next_delivery_quantity'),
    65→
    66→    // Cost
    67→    unitCost: decimal('unit_cost', { precision: 12, scale: 2 }),
    68→    currency: varchar('currency', { length: 3 }).default('SGD'),
    69→
    70→    // Sync tracking
    71→    lastSyncAt: timestamp('last_sync_at', { withTimezone: true }),
    72→    sourceSystem: varchar('source_system', { length: 50 }).default('MAXIMO'),
    73→
    74→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    75→  },
    76→  (table) => [
    77→    uniqueIndex('idx_inventory_depot_part').on(table.depotId, table.partNumber),
    78→    index('idx_inventory_depot').on(table.depotId),
    79→    index('idx_inventory_part').on(table.partNumber),
    80→  ]
    81→);
    82→
    83→/**
    84→ * Material Reservations - Links inventory to PM schedules
    85→ *
    86→ * Two reservation types:
    87→ * - SOFT: Tentative, auto-expires if PM moves (90-day soft constraint)
    88→ * - HARD: Committed, parts allocated (14-day hard constraint)
    89→ *
    90→ * Constraint enforcement:
    91→ * - BLOCKING parts with SOFT reservation: Warning, PM can proceed
    92→ * - BLOCKING parts without reservation: PM cannot be scheduled
    93→ */
    94→export const materialReservations = pgTable(
    95→  'material_reservations',
    96→  {
    97→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    98→    pmScheduleId: uuid('pm_schedule_id')
    99→      .notNull()
   100→      .references(() => pmSchedule.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }),
   101→    inventoryPositionId: uuid('inventory_position_id')
   102→      .notNull()
   103→      .references(() => inventoryPositions.id),
   104→    partNumber: varchar('part_number', { length: 50 }).notNull(),
   105→
   106→    quantityReserved: integer('quantity_reserved').notNull(),
   107→    reservationType: reservationTypeEnum('reservation_type').default('SOFT'),
   108→
   109→    reservedAt: timestamp('reserved_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   110→    reservedBy: uuid('reserved_by').references(() => users.id),
   111→    expiresAt: timestamp('expires_at', { withTimezone: true }), // SOFT reservations auto-expire
   112→
   113→    // Fulfillment
   114→    fulfilledAt: timestamp('fulfilled_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   115→    fulfilledQuantity: integer('fulfilled_quantity'),
   116→  },
   117→  (table) => [
   118→    uniqueIndex('idx_reservations_pm_part').on(table.pmScheduleId, table.partNumber),
   119→    index('idx_reservations_pm').on(table.pmScheduleId),
   120→    index('idx_reservations_inventory').on(table.inventoryPositionId),
   121→    index('idx_reservations_expiring').on(table.expiresAt),
   122→  ]
   123→);
   124→
   125→// ============================================================================
   126→// Relations
   127→// ============================================================================
   128→
   129→export const inventoryPositionsRelations = relations(inventoryPositions, ({ one, many }) => ({
   130→  depot: one(depots, {
   131→    fields: [inventoryPositions.depotId],
   132→    references: [depots.id],
   133→  }),
   134→  reservations: many(materialReservations),
   135→}));
   136→
   137→export const materialReservationsRelations = relations(materialReservations, ({ one }) => ({
   138→  pmScheduleRecord: one(pmSchedule, {
   139→    fields: [materialReservations.pmScheduleId],
   140→    references: [pmSchedule.id],
   141→  }),
   142→  inventoryPosition: one(inventoryPositions, {
   143→    fields: [materialReservations.inventoryPositionId],
   144→    references: [inventoryPositions.id],
   145→  }),
   146→  reservedByUser: one(users, {
   147→    fields: [materialReservations.reservedBy],
   148→    references: [users.id],
   149→  }),
   150→}));
   151→

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Man-hour Planning Tables
     3→ *
     4→ * Workforce capacity planning and workload leveling.
     5→ * Used to balance maintenance workload across time periods
     6→ * and prevent overloading/underutilization of depot resources.
     7→ *
     8→ * Leveling strategies (priority order):
     9→ * 1. ADVANCE_PM (1.1x cost, LOW risk)
    10→ * 2. DEFER_PM (1.05x cost, LOW risk)
    11→ * 3. ADD_OVERTIME (1.5x cost, NO risk)
    12→ * 4. CROSS_DEPOT (1.3x cost, MEDIUM risk)
    13→ * 5. ADD_CONTRACTOR (2.0x cost, MEDIUM risk)
    14→ * 6. MANUAL_REVIEW (flagged for high unresolvable overload)
    15→ *
    16→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.7
    17→ */
    18→
    19→import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    20→import {
    21→  date,
    22→  decimal,
    23→  index,
    24→  integer,
    25→  pgTable,
    26→  text,
    27→  timestamp,
    28→  uniqueIndex,
    29→  uuid,
    30→  varchar,
    31→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    32→import { depots } from './core-fleet';
    33→import { adjustmentStatusEnum } from './enums';
    34→import { users } from './users';
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Man-hour Capacity - Resource availability by depot
    38→ *
    39→ * Defines the workforce capacity for each depot by shift type.
    40→ * Includes utilization targets to guide leveling decisions.
    41→ */
    42→export const manhourCapacity = pgTable(
    43→  'manhour_capacity',
    44→  {
    45→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    46→    depotId: uuid('depot_id')
    47→      .notNull()
    48→      .references(() => depots.id),
    49→    effectiveFrom: date('effective_from').notNull(),
    50→    effectiveTo: date('effective_to'),
    51→
    52→    // Base capacity per shift type (hours per week)
    53→    dayShiftHours: decimal('day_shift_hours', { precision: 8, scale: 2 }).notNull(),
    54→    nightShiftHours: decimal('night_shift_hours', { precision: 8, scale: 2 }).default('0'),
    55→    weekendHours: decimal('weekend_hours', { precision: 8, scale: 2 }).default('0'),
    56→
    57→    // Staff counts
    58→    techniciansDay: integer('technicians_day').notNull(),
    59→    techniciansNight: integer('technicians_night').default(0),
    60→    specialists: integer('specialists').default(0),
    61→
    62→    // Utilization targets
    63→    maxOvertimePct: decimal('max_overtime_pct', { precision: 5, scale: 2 }).default('20.0'),
    64→    minUtilizationPct: decimal('min_utilization_pct', { precision: 5, scale: 2 }).default('70.0'),
    65→    targetUtilizationPct: decimal('target_utilization_pct', { precision: 5, scale: 2 }).default(
    66→      '85.0'
    67→    ),
    68→
    69→    notes: text('notes'),
    70→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    71→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    72→  },
    73→  (table) => [
    74→    uniqueIndex('idx_manhour_cap_depot_date').on(table.depotId, table.effectiveFrom),
    75→    index('idx_manhour_cap_depot').on(table.depotId),
    76→  ]
    77→);
    78→
    79→/**
    80→ * Man-hour Projections - Generated workload forecasts
    81→ *
    82→ * Created by the leveling engine to show required vs. available hours.
    83→ * Used for capacity planning and overload detection.
    84→ */
    85→export const manhourProjections = pgTable(
    86→  'manhour_projections',
    87→  {
    88→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    89→    projectionRunId: uuid('projection_run_id').notNull(),
    90→    depotId: uuid('depot_id')
    91→      .notNull()
    92→      .references(() => depots.id),
    93→
    94→    periodStart: date('period_start').notNull(),
    95→    periodEnd: date('period_end').notNull(),
    96→    granularity: varchar('granularity', { length: 20 }).notNull(), // 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY'
    97→
    98→    // Required hours breakdown
    99→    requiredPmHours: decimal('required_pm_hours', { precision: 10, scale: 2 }).notNull(),
   100→    requiredCorrectiveHours: decimal('required_corrective_hours', {
   101→      precision: 10,
   102→      scale: 2,
   103→    }).default('0'),
   104→    requiredOverhaulHours: decimal('required_overhaul_hours', {
   105→      precision: 10,
   106→      scale: 2,
   107→    }).default('0'),
   108→    requiredTotalHours: decimal('required_total_hours', { precision: 10, scale: 2 }).notNull(),
   109→
   110→    // Available hours
   111→    availableRegularHours: decimal('available_regular_hours', {
   112→      precision: 10,
   113→      scale: 2,
   114→    }).notNull(),
   115→    availableOvertimeHours: decimal('available_overtime_hours', {
   116→      precision: 10,
   117→      scale: 2,
   118→    }).notNull(),
   119→    availableTotalHours: decimal('available_total_hours', { precision: 10, scale: 2 }).notNull(),
   120→
   121→    // Computed metrics (calculated in application layer)
   122→    // variance_hours = available_total - required_total
   123→    // utilization_pct = (required_total / available_total) * 100
   124→    // is_overloaded = required_total > available_total
   125→    // is_underutilized = required_total < available_total * 0.7
   126→
   127→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   128→  },
   129→  (table) => [
   130→    index('idx_manhour_proj_depot').on(table.depotId, table.periodStart),
   131→    index('idx_manhour_proj_run').on(table.projectionRunId),
   132→  ]
   133→);
   134→
   135→/**
   136→ * Man-hour Adjustments - Proposed workload changes
   137→ *
   138→ * Leveling engine suggestions to balance workload.
   139→ * Requires approval workflow before being applied.
   140→ */
   141→export const manhourAdjustments = pgTable(
   142→  'manhour_adjustments',
   143→  {
   144→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   145→    projectionId: uuid('projection_id')
   146→      .notNull()
   147→      .references(() => manhourProjections.id),
   148→
   149→    adjustmentType: varchar('adjustment_type', { length: 50 }).notNull(),
   150→    // 'ADD_OVERTIME', 'DEFER_PM', 'ADVANCE_PM', 'CROSS_DEPOT', 'ADD_CONTRACTOR'
   151→
   152→    sourcePeriodStart: date('source_period_start'),
   153→    sourcePeriodEnd: date('source_period_end'),
   154→    targetPeriodStart: date('target_period_start'),
   155→    targetPeriodEnd: date('target_period_end'),
   156→    hoursAdjusted: decimal('hours_adjusted', { precision: 10, scale: 2 }).notNull(),
   157→
   158→    affectedPmIds: text('affected_pm_ids').array().default([]),
   159→    affectedTrainIds: text('affected_train_ids').array().default([]),
   160→
   161→    // Impact assessment
   162→    costImpact: decimal('cost_impact', { precision: 12, scale: 2 }),
   163→    riskImpact: varchar('risk_impact', { length: 20 }), // 'NONE', 'LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'
   164→    riskDescription: text('risk_description'),
   165→
   166→    // Workflow
   167→    status: adjustmentStatusEnum('status').default('PROPOSED'),
   168→    proposedBy: uuid('proposed_by').references(() => users.id),
   169→    proposedAt: timestamp('proposed_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   170→    approvedBy: uuid('approved_by').references(() => users.id),
   171→    approvedAt: timestamp('approved_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   172→    rejectionReason: text('rejection_reason'),
   173→
   174→    // Explanation
   175→    reason: text('reason').notNull(),
   176→
   177→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   178→  },
   179→  (table) => [
   180→    index('idx_manhour_adj_projection').on(table.projectionId),
   181→    index('idx_manhour_adj_status').on(table.status),
   182→  ]
   183→);
   184→
   185→// ============================================================================
   186→// Relations
   187→// ============================================================================
   188→
   189→export const manhourCapacityRelations = relations(manhourCapacity, ({ one }) => ({
   190→  depot: one(depots, {

   194→}));

   196→export const manhourProjectionsRelations = relations(manhourProjections, ({ one, many }) => ({
